pi_items.source: Verified consistency across task.json, setup_task.sh, and export_result.sh. All scripts use identical OUTPUT_PATH variable.
Verified against AutoCAD Color Index (ACI) standard. ACI 3 = Green. Confirmed via AutoCAD documentation and ezdxf library documentation.
Verified against AutoCAD Color Index (ACI) standard. ACI 1 = Red. Confirmed via AutoCAD documentation and ezdxf library documentation.
Verified against AutoCAD Color Index (ACI) standard. ACI 7 = White. Confirmed via AutoCAD documentation and ezdxf library documentation.
Verified against task.json description: 'Row 1: (0, 0)' with dimensions '10000mm wide × 2000mm tall'. Identical match.
Verified against task.json description: 'Row 2: (0, 4000)' with dimensions '10000mm wide × 2000mm tall'. Identical match.
Verified against task.json description: 'Row 3: (0, 8000)' with dimensions '10000mm wide × 2000mm tall'. Identical match.
Verified against task.json description: 'Row 4: (0, 12000)' with dimensions '10000mm wide × 2000mm tall'. Identical match.

Verified against task.json description: 'Combiner Box: rectangle 600mm × 600mm with lower-left corner at (11000, 6700)'. Identical match. Also verified in verifier.py line 125: find_rect(11000, 6700, 600, 600, 'ELECTRICAL').

Verified against task.json description: 'Site Boundary: rectangle from (-2000, -2000) to (14000, 16000) — that is, 16000mm wide × 18000mm tall with lower-left corner at (-2000, -2000)'. Identical match. Verified calculation: upper-right = (-2000 + 16000, -2000 + 18000) = (14000, 16000).

Verified from task.json description which specifies 4 lines: 'Draw 4 lines connecting the right-center of each panel row to the center of the combiner box'. Also confirmed in verifier.py which checks for 4 conduit lines connecting hub at (11300, 7000).
Verified from task.json description: 'connect...to the center of the combiner box at (11300, 7000)'. Also confirmed in verifier.py line 146: hub_point = (11300, 7000).

Verified from task.json description: 'Save the drawing as: ~/Documents/LibreCAD/solar_array_plan.dxf'. DXF is AutoCAD Drawing Interchange Format, a standard CAD file format. LibreCAD fully supports DXF format for both reading and writing.

The task does not use external datasets or real-world data. It is a specification-driven task where the agent creates a CAD drawing from geometric specifications. No external data files are referenced or required (other than the LibreCAD application itself).

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic CAD design task for LibreCAD where the agent creates a solar array site plan from geometric specifications. The task requires creating: (1) four panel rows (10000×2000 mm each) on the PANELS layer with ACI color 3 (Green), placed at y-coordinates 0, 4000, 8000, and 12000; (2) a combiner box (600×600 mm) on the ELECTRICAL layer with ACI color 1 (Red) at coordinates (11000, 6700); (3) four conduit lines on the ELECTRICAL layer connecting panel row endpoints to a hub point at (11300, 7000); and (4) a site boundary rectangle (16000×18000 mm) on the BORDER layer with ACI color 7 (White) at lower-left corner (-2000, -2000). The output must be saved as a DXF file at /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/solar_array_plan.dxf. All geometric specifications in the metadata match exactly with the task description and verifier expectations.
